engineGuide.h2: Volvo V40 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Volvo V40 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Volvo's flagship VEA twin-turbo diesel for V40 D4; high performance but timing chain and turbo concerns drive UK replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: D4, D4 Business, D4 Momentum, D4 R-Design, D4 SE, D4 Cross Country
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear (60k-100k miles)
Turbo oil seal leakage under high load
EGR cooler clogging on urban driving cycles
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T14 Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level VEA diesel replacing older units; improved emissions but retains timing chain vulnerability in high-mileage examples.
compatibleVariants: D3, D3 Business, D3 Momentum, D3 R-Design, D3 SE
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch beyond 80k miles
DPF regeneration issues on short journeys
Injector seal failure causing diesel smell
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T9 Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Early VEA diesel for D2/D3; cost-effective but shares EGR and turbo failure patterns with later VEA units.
compatibleVariants: D2, D2 Business, D2 Momentum, D3 (early)
commonFailures: EGR valve sticking and carbon buildup
Turbo actuator failure at higher mileage
Coolant hose deterioration (recall-related)
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T8 Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel (PSA-derived)
entries.history: PSA-sourced 1.6L diesel used in early V40 D2; economical but prone to EGR and injector issues common to the DV6 family.
commonFailures: EGR valve failure causing limp mode
Injector seal leaks and diesel odour
Turbo wastegate sticking on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4162T Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: Volvo's 1.5L three-cylinder Drive-E petrol; efficient and punchy but carbon buildup and balance shaft concerns affect longevity.
compatibleVariants: T3, T3 Business, T3 Momentum, T3 R-Design, T3 SE
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection)
Balance shaft bearing wear causing vibration
Turbo oil feed pipe leakage
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4154T4 Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: Volvo's versatile 2.0L VEA petrol for T4; strong performance but coolant system and turbo issues drive replacement enquiries.
compatibleVariants: T4, T4 Business, T4 Momentum, T4 R-Design, T4 Cross Country
commonFailures: Coolant leak at thermostat housing
Turbo wastegate rattle and boost loss
PCV system failure causing oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204T19 Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L High-Output Turbo Petrol
entries.history: High-performance variant of the 2.0L VEA for T5; exceptional power but increased thermal stress accelerates component wear.
compatibleVariants: T5, T5 Momentum, T5 R-Design, T5 Cross Country
commonFailures: Piston ring wear under high load
Turbo bearing failure on aggressive driving
Oil consumption due to PCV issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204T41 Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: Volvo's iconic 5-cylinder turbo petrol; beloved for character but high-mileage units suffer piston and turbo wear.
commonFailures: Piston ring wear and oil consumption
Turbo oil seal leakage
Timing belt tensioner failure if not serviced
entries.cta: Get quotes for B5254T12 Volvo V40 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Volvo V40 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volvo V40's VEA diesel and Drive-E petrol engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with timing chain, turbo and EGR issues most frequently reported. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to weigh repair vs replacement options for your specific variant.

problems.group: Volvo V40 D4 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Volvo V40 D4 Timing Chain Failure - Why VEA Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Rear-mounted timing chain tensioner wears prematurely, allowing chain slack that causes rattling noise and potential valve damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; not suitable once rattling starts

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace damaged valves, re-time engine
repairOptions.longevity: Only if chain jumped but block undamaged; high risk of recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt D4204T14 with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution; eliminates original failure point

problems.recommendation: On a Volvo V40 D4 worth £4,000-£8,000, preventative chain replacement at 60k miles offers best value. Once rattling starts, replacement with a rebuilt unit is often more economical than major repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt D4204T14 engine prices for your Volvo V40

problems.group: Volvo V40 D2/D3 EGR Valve Failure
problems.h4: Volvo V40 D2/D3 EGR Valve Failure - Diesel Limp Mode Fix
problems.rootCause: EGR valve clogs with carbon deposits from urban driving, restricting exhaust flow and triggering limp mode to protect the engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean or replace EGR valve; reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Effective short-term fix; carbon may rebuild without driving pattern change

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Forced DPF regeneration, EGR delete software (where legal), intake cleaning
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for high-mileage urban cars; check MOT compliance first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt D2/D3 engine with updated EGR components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has additional wear; eliminates multiple failure points

problems.recommendation: For isolated EGR failure under 70k miles, cleaning or replacement is cost-effective. If combined with turbo or DPF issues, a rebuilt engine often delivers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Volvo V40 D2 D3 engine replacement

problems.h4: Volvo V40 T3/T4 Turbo Failure - Petrol Boost Loss & Oil Leaks
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil feed pipe or seal deteriorates, causing oil leakage, boost pressure loss, and potential engine damage from oil starvation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo oil feed pipe, seals, gaskets; flush oil system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if turbo internals undamaged; requires prompt attention

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit refurbished turbo unit; update oil lines and coolant hoses
repairOptions.longevity: Good option if block is healthy; ensure oil changes are maintained

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt T3/T4 engine with upgraded turbo components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if engine shows additional wear or high mileage

problems.recommendation: Catch turbo oil leaks early for affordable repair. If boost loss is accompanied by knocking or excessive oil consumption, replacement with a rebuilt unit is the safer investment.
problems.cta: Compare Volvo V40 T3 T4 petrol engine prices

problems.h4: Volvo V40 Coolant Leak & Overheating - Recall-Related Engine Damage
problems.affectedModels: Volvo V40 D2, D3, D4, T3, T4 (2012-2019, multiple codes)
problems.rootCause: Plastic coolant hose or thermostat housing cracks (subject to Volvo recalls R/2018/377, R/2019/180), causing coolant loss, overheating, and potential head gasket failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cracked hose/thermostat housing; refill and bleed cooling system
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative fix; check recall status first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, machine surfaces if warped
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged; requires specialist assessment

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated cooling components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if overheating caused warping or seizure

problems.recommendation: Check your V40's recall status immediately. If overheating occurred, have a compression test done; if results are poor, replacement is more reliable than repair.
problems.cta: Get Volvo V40 engine replacement quotes with recall-compliant parts

problems.h4: Volvo V40 DPF Clogging - Diesel Particulate Filter Failure
problems.affectedModels: Volvo V40 D2, D3, D4 (2012-2019, all diesel codes)
problems.rootCause: Frequent short journeys prevent DPF regeneration, causing soot accumulation that restricts exhaust flow, increases backpressure, and damages turbo.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic tool initiates active DPF regeneration; clear fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if clogging is mild; requires motorway driving afterwards

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit new or refurbished DPF unit; update ECU software
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for heavily clogged units; ensure EGR/turbo are healthy first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt diesel engine with updated emissions components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if DPF failure caused turbo or engine damage

problems.recommendation: For isolated DPF clogging under 80k miles, regeneration or replacement is cost-effective. If combined with turbo failure or excessive oil consumption, a rebuilt engine offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Compare Volvo V40 diesel engine replacement prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volvo V40
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing timing chain rattles, EGR limp mode, turbo leaks, coolant damage or DPF clogging, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volvo V40 specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volvo V40 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volvo V40 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.description: Volvo's VEA diesel family powers most UK V40s, offering strong economy and torque but requiring attention to timing chain, turbo and emissions system maintenance.
items.descriptor: Volvo's VEA diesel family powers most UK V40s, offering strong economy and torque but requiring attention to timing chain, turbo and emissions system maintenance.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volvo V40 Diesel Engine Families
families: D4204T14 - D4 twin-turbo flagship, high performance but timing chain concerns
D4204T9 - D3 mid-range unit, balanced performance and reliability
D4204T8 / D4162T - Early D2/D3 units, economical but EGR-prone
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and low CO2 emissions
Timing chain tensioner wear on VEA units between 60k-100k miles
High UK replacement demand on D3/D4 variants due to urban driving patterns
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo V40 Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Volvo V40 Petrol Engines
items.description: Volvo's Drive-E petrol family delivers punchy performance and modern efficiency across V40 T3, T4 and T5 variants, though direct injection requires periodic maintenance.
items.descriptor: Volvo's Drive-E petrol family delivers punchy performance and modern efficiency across V40 T3, T4 and T5 variants, though direct injection requires periodic maintenance.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volvo V40 Petrol Engine Families
families: B4154T4 - 1.5L three-cylinder T3, efficient but carbon-prone
B4204T19 - 2.0L T4, versatile performer with coolant system vigilance needed
B4204T41 / B5254T12 - High-output T5 units, powerful but thermally stressed
knownFor: Strong acceleration and refined character across the range
Carbon buildup on direct injection intake valves at higher mileage
Turbo oil seal and coolant system issues on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Volvo V40 Petrol engine prices

items.title: Volvo V40 Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: Late-production V40 variants introduced 48V mild-hybrid technology for improved efficiency, though replacement considerations differ from standard engines.
items.descriptor: Late-production V40 variants introduced 48V mild-hybrid technology for improved efficiency, though replacement considerations differ from standard engines.
families: B4154T6 - 1.5L T3 MHEV (late production, limited UK availability)
48-volt starter-generator recovers braking energy for electric torque assistance
Enables smoother stop-start and slight fuel economy improvement
Battery health check recommended during engine replacement
importantNotes: MHEV variants were rare in UK V40 range; most are standard petrol/diesel
Engine replacement requires hybrid system inspection by qualified specialist
Availability of MHEV-specific rebuilt units is more limited than standard engines

engineYears.h2: Volvo V40 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Volvo V40's production timeline (2012-2019), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Launch Year: PSA Diesel & Early Petrol
years.description: The V40 launched with PSA-sourced 1.6L diesel and Ford-derived 1.6L petrol engines, establishing the model's economical positioning.
keyChanges: New compact platform replacing S40/V50 architecture
Introduction of D2 (1.6L PSA diesel) and T3 (1.6L EcoBoost petrol)
City Safety autonomous braking standard across range
knownFor: Strong launch appeal with class-leading safety tech
PSA diesel EGR issues emerging on early high-mileage examples
EcoBoost petrol timing chain concerns on neglected units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012 Volvo V40 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo V40 D2 engine replacement • Volvo V40 1.6 diesel EGR fix • Volvo V40 T3 timing chain cost
enquiries: Volvo V40 T3 timing chain cost

years.description: Volvo introduced its in-house VEA diesel family, replacing PSA units and establishing the D3/D4 variants that would dominate UK sales.
keyChanges: Launch of 2.0L VEA diesel engines (D3, D4)
Introduction of T5 2.5L five-cylinder petrol flagship
Minor interior updates and new trim levels
knownFor: VEA diesels offer improved refinement and economy vs PSA units
Early VEA timing chain tensioner concerns emerging on high-mileage D4
T5 five-cylinder beloved for character but thermally stressed
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2014 Volvo V40 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo V40 D4 timing chain replacement • Volvo V40 T5 engine rebuild cost • Volvo V40 D3 turbo failure
enquiries: Volvo V40 D4 timing chain replacement
Volvo V40 T5 engine rebuild cost

years.description: The facelifted V40 introduced Volvo's Drive-E petrol family and updated styling, establishing the engine lineup that would run to end of production.
keyChanges: Introduction of 1.5L and 2.0L Drive-E petrol engines (T3, T4)
Exterior refresh with Thor's Hammer DRLs
Updated infotainment and driver assistance features
knownFor: Drive-E petrols deliver strong performance with improved efficiency
Carbon buildup on direct injection intake valves noted on T3/T4
D4 remains high-demand replacement due to urban driving patterns
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2016 Volvo V40 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo V40 T3 carbon clean • Volvo V40 T4 coolant leak fix • Volvo V40 D4 turbo replacement
enquiries: Volvo V40 T4 coolant leak fix

years.description: Final V40 production years saw minor updates and limited MHEV introduction, with established engine families continuing unchanged.
keyChanges: Limited introduction of 48V mild-hybrid technology (T3 MHEV)
knownFor: Mature engine families with well-documented failure patterns
MHEV variants rare in UK; most replacements remain standard petrol/diesel
Strong used market demand keeps replacement engine enquiries high
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2019 Volvo V40 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo V40 2019 engine replacement • Volvo V40 MHEV specialist • Volvo V40 D4 rebuilt engine warranty
enquiries: Volvo V40 D4 rebuilt engine warranty

faq.h2: Volvo V40 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Volvo V40 engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Volvo V40 engine replacement costs typically range from £1,450 for a basic D2 rebuilt unit to £7,500 for a T5 performance engine, depending on variant, engine code and condition type chosen.
keyPoints: Diesel D2/D3 rebuilt engines average £1,450-£3,600 supply only
Petrol T3/T4 rebuilt units typically £1,650-£4,200 supply only
Performance T5 and Cross Country variants command premium pricing
Supply & fit adds £450-£950 to engine price depending on garage
items.cta: Get a tailored Volvo V40 engine quote in 60 seconds

items.question: What is the most common Volvo V40 engine problem?
items.answer: Timing chain tensioner wear on VEA diesel engines (D3/D4) is the most frequently reported Volvo V40 engine failure, typically occurring between 60,000-100,000 miles.
keyPoints: D4204T14 D4 engines most affected by timing chain issues
Rattling noise on cold start is the key warning symptom
Ignoring symptoms risks valve damage and catastrophic failure
Rebuilt engines include upgraded tensioners to address original weakness
items.cta: Compare rebuilt D4204T14 engine prices for your Volvo V40

items.question: How do I find my Volvo V40 engine code?
items.answer: Your Volvo V40 engine code is stamped on the engine block, listed in your V5C logbook under section D.2, or instantly verified by entering your registration on EngineMarket.
keyPoints: Common codes: D4204T14 (D4), D4204T9 (D3), B4154T4 (T3)
DVLA lookup via registration is fastest and most reliable method
Engine code is essential for ensuring correct replacement compatibility
Our specialists verify code match before dispatch to prevent fitment errors
items.cta: Instantly identify your Volvo V40 engine code by registration

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Volvo V40 engine?
items.answer: If your Volvo V40 engine has suffered catastrophic failure (seized, thrown rod, severe overheating damage), replacement with a rebuilt unit is typically more cost-effective than major repairs.
keyPoints: Minor issues like EGR or turbo seals may be repairable under £1,000
Major internal damage usually exceeds £3,000 for dealer repairs
Rebuilt engines offer warranty protection repairs cannot match
Consider vehicle value: replacement rarely justified if V40 worth under £2,000
items.cta: Get repair vs replacement advice for your Volvo V40 engine

items.question: What warranty comes with Volvo V40 replacement engines?
items.answer: All Volvo V40 engines supplied through EngineMarket include a Volvomum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty as standard, with extended options available from premium suppliers.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Unlimited mileage terms apply across UK mainland
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare Volvo V40 engine warranty options from UK specialists

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Volvo V40?
items.answer: Engine swaps between different Volvo V40 codes are technically complex and often not cost-effective; we recommend matching your original engine code for reliable, warranty-backed replacement.
keyPoints: ECU, wiring loom and transmission compatibility must be verified
MOT emissions compliance may be affected by non-original engines
Specialist assessment required before attempting any engine swap
Our network specialists can advise on feasible upgrade options
items.cta: Get expert advice on Volvo V40 engine compatibility

items.question: How long does Volvo V40 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only Volvo V40 engines typically dispatch within 1-3 working days; full supply & fit services usually complete within 3-5 working days depending on garage availability.
keyPoints: Next-day delivery available for urgent requirements (subject to stock)
Supply & fit timing depends on local garage schedule and complexity
Allow extra time for recall-compliant parts if applicable
Our team coordinates delivery and fitting for seamless service
items.cta: Book Volvo V40 engine supply & fit with guaranteed turnaround

items.question: Are Volvo V40 replacement engines reliable?
items.answer: Rebuilt Volvo V40 engines from EngineMarket's vetted specialists undergo rigorous testing and include upgraded components addressing known failure points, delivering reliability equal to or exceeding original specification.
keyPoints: All units bench-tested for compression, oil pressure and leaks
Upgraded timing chains, tensioners and turbo components where relevant
12-month unlimited mileage warranty provides peace of mind
Supplier ratings and reviews help you choose trusted partners

items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket's network of Volvo V40 engine specialists covers all UK mainland postcodes, with nationwide delivery and local supply & fit options available.

Supply & fit services available through vetted local garages nationwide
Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland included in coverage
Remote locations may incur additional delivery charges
